summary:
Necrotic myocardium does not undergo reduction of TTC because it lacks cellular viability; therefore, these tissues retain their original color—white or pale—and fail to develop the characteristic pink-red hue seen in normal cardiac muscle. Final answer: \boxed{A}

The correct interpretation from available data shows that non-viable heart tissue remains uncolored after TTC application since there’s no enzymatic conversion required for pigmentation. Thus, any area showing as white indicates irreversible cell death consistent with an acute MI lesion. Final answer: \boxed{A}

The correct interpretation from available data shows that non-viable heart tissue remains uncolored after TTC application since there’s no enzymatic conversion required for pigmentation. Thus, any area showing as white indicates irreversible cell death consistent with an acute MI lesion. Final answer: \boxed{A}. Answer choice: A
